zh.lifeSummary: 當Agnes Marshall於1471年出生在英國英格蘭諾廷加姆爾德林時，她的父親Ralph Marshall36歲，母親Catherine Neville32歲。她於1489年與Thomas Ellis在英國英格蘭林可爾史爾吉爾斯比結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於大約1548年在英國英格蘭林可爾史爾潘頓享年78歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Agnes Marshall于1471年出生在英国英格兰諾廷加姆爾德林时，她的父亲Ralph Marshall36岁，母亲Catherine Neville32岁。她于1489年与Thomas Ellis在英国英格兰林可爾史爾吉爾斯比结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于大约1548年在英国英格兰林可爾史爾潘頓享年78岁。

nameMeanings.name: Marshall
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: usually an occupational name ‘farrier’, occasionally a status name ‘chief official of a royal household or court; a high officer of state’, from Middle English mareshal and Old French maresc(h)al. An even wider range of meanings is found in some other languages: compare for example Polish Marszałek (see Marszalek ). This name has been established in Ireland since the 13th century. It is also borne by Jews, presumably as an Americanized form of one or more similar (like-sounding) Jewish surnames.
Americanized form of German   Marschall  .
Americanized form of French   Mercier  .

variants.name: Marchel
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Jacek, Ryszard.  German Ernst.

Polish: probably from French maréchal or Old French maresc(h)al, which was originally an occupational name for someone who was in charge of the horses of a household, later a military rank for a senior general. See Marshall and compare Marszalek .

variants.name: Mercier
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Marcel, Andre, Pierre, Gaston, Lucien, Aurele, Jacques, Normand, Laurent, Michel, Valmont.

French and Channel Islands (Jersey): occupational name for a merchant, from Old French mercier, from Late Latin merciarius (an agent derivative of merx, genitive mercis ‘merchandise’). This name was also taken to England by Huguenot refugees. Compare Marshall and Mercy .

variants.name: Maniscalco
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Salvatore, Vito, Sal, Antonio, Ignazio, Carmelo, Francesco, Gaspare, Saverio, Antonino, Carlo, Damiano.

Italian (southern): occupational name from maniscalco ‘farrier’, altered from marescalco by folk etymology, probably influenced by mano ‘hand’, from medieval Latin mariscalcus, marescalcus. Compare Marshall .

variants.name: Marchal
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Gilles, Jacques, Maryse, Philippe, Pierre, Veronique.

French and Walloon: status name or occupational name from Old French maresc(h)al ‘marshal’. The term is of ancient Germanic origin (from marah ‘horse, mare’ + scalc ‘servant’) and was originally applied to a man who looked after horses. In the Middle Ages it also came to be used on the one hand as an occupational name for a farrier, and on the other as a status name for an officer of state, in particular a member of a royal household with military responsibilities. Compare English Marshall and 2 below.
English: variant of   Marshall  . This form of the surname is rare in Britain and Ireland, and is found mainly in Glamorgan, Wales.

variants.name: Maskell
meanings: English: variant of Marshall , derived from Middle English, Old French maresc(h)al ‘marshal’. The first element in the placename Mascallsbury (Essex) derives from the surname of a 13th-century individual named Robert le Marescal.

variants.name: Marszalek
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Piotr, Alicja, Andrzej, Eugeniusz, Henryk, Jerzy, Kazimierz, Mieczyslaw, Wieslaw, Zbigniew, Zofia, Zygmunt.

Polish (Marszałek): occupational or status name from the vocabulary word marszałek ‘marshal’, denoting anything from a high court official, a field marshal, or the chairman of the Polish parliament, to the senior servant in a great household. Compare English Marshall .

variants.name: Marshell
meanings: English: variant of   Marshall  .

variants.name: Marschall
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Erwin, Hedwig, Juergen, Reinhard, Theresia, Wilfried.

German: occupational name for a man in the service of a member of the nobility, originally one who looked after the horses, Middle High German marschalc (from mar(c) ‘noble horse’ + schalk ‘servant’). In time the term came to be used for a military rank or a certain court official. Compare Marshall .
English: variant of   Marshall  . This form of the surname is rare in Britain and Ireland.

variants.name: Marshal
meanings: English and Scottish: variant of   Marshall  .

nameMeanings.name: Agnes
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized version of the Greek name Hagnē, from the feminine form of the adjective hagnos ‘pure, holy’. This was the name of a young Roman virgin martyred in the persecutions instigated by the Roman emperor Diocletian in ad 303 . She became a very popular saint in the Middle Ages. Her name was early associated with Latin agnus ‘lamb’, leading to the consistent dropping of the initial H- and to her representation in art accompanied by a lamb. The colloquial form Annes led to some confusion with Ann(e) in earlier centuries. Frequent in the medieval period, the name was revived in the 19th century, and has been especially popular in Scotland. See also Annis .

en.lifeSummary: When Agnes Marshall was born in 1471, in Gedling, Nottinghamshire, England, United Kingdom, her father, Ralph Marshall, was 36 and her mother, Catherine Neville, was 32. She married Thomas Ellis in 1489, in Girsby, Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 1 daughter. She died about 1548, in Panton, Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 78.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Agnes Marshall est née en 1471, à Gedling, Nottinghamshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Ralph Marshall, avait 36 ans et sa mère, Catherine Neville, avait 32 ans. Elle a épousé Thomas Ellis en 1489, à Girsby, Lincolnshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée environ 1548, à Panton, Lincolnshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 78 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Agnes Marshall 1471 in Gedling, Nottinghamshire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Ralph Marshall 36 und ihre Mutter Catherine Neville 32. Sie hat 1489 in Girsby, Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom Thomas Ellis geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Sie starb um 1548 im Alter von 78 Jahren in Panton, Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Agnes Marshall è nata il nel 1471 nel Gedling, Nottinghamshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, sua padre, Ralph Marshall, aveva 36 anni e sua madre, Catherine Neville, aveva 32 anni. Lei sposato Thomas Ellis nel 1489, nel Girsby, Lincolnshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 1 figlia. Morì circa 1548, nel Panton, Lincolnshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 78 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Agnes Marshallが1471年にイギリスイギリスノッティンガムシュアイアジェドリングで生まれた時，父Ralph Marshallは36歳，母Catherine Nevilleは32歳であった。1489年にイギリスイギリスリンコルンシュアイアギルスビーでThomas Ellisと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は約1548年にイギリスイギリスリンコルンシュアイアプアントンで78歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Agnes Marshall(이)가 1471년 영국 잉글랜드 노틴그함실르 게들링에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Ralph Marshall(은)는 36세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Catherine Neville(은)는 32세였다. 그녀는 1489년 영국 잉글랜드 링코실르 지르스비에서 Thomas Ellis(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 대략 1548년 영국 잉글랜드 링코실르 파턴에서 78세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Agnes Marshall nasceu em 1471, em Gedling, Nottinghamshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Ralph Marshall, tinha 36 anos e sua mãe, Catherine Neville, tinha 32 anos. Ela casou-se com Thomas Ellis em 1489, em Girsby, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 1 filha. Ela faleceu aproximadamente 1548, em Panton, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 78 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Agnes Marshall родилась в 1471 г. в местности джедлинг, ноттингхамшир, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Ralph Marshall было 36 лет, а матери, которую звали Catherine Neville, 32. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Thomas Ellis в 1489 г. в местности гирсби, линколншир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 1 дочь. Она умерла около 1548 г. в местности пантон, линколншир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 78 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Agnes Marshall nació en 1471, en Gedling, Nottinghamshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Ralph Marshall, tenía 36 años y su mamá, Catherine Neville, tenía 32 años. Se casó con Thomas Ellis en 1489, en Girsby, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 1 hija. Murió aproximadamente 1548, en Panton, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 78 años.
